title: CMake verilate function, move execution of verilator to build phase from configure phase
body:
Hello,

I am using CMake as my build system for RTL simulations.
Very often I have a scenario where I have a custom command that would generate Verilog files.
I am using custom_commands rather than execute_process, as I prefer that my files are generated during build phase, so I can benefit from dependency management.

I am writing this issue to ask if anyone else has a better way of solving this problem, or if my solution is good enough I can make a pull request.

https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/master/verilator-config.cmake.in#L140
I am using function verilate linked above.
The problem with this is that function verilate expect that files in SOURCES argument exist at time of cmake configuration.

Then verilate proceeds to generate cmake file with the list of Cpp sources:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/master/verilator-config.cmake.in#L225
The list of cpp files is generated at configure time, and I assume that this list depends on the content of verilog files.
Which breaks my first ideas to solve this problem, that is:

## First solution
Generate dummy verilog files during configure phase, so verilator doesnt fail looking for files, but it also need to have proper ports and parameters so it can be instantiated.
I was doing this initially, but it would happen that if the files become drastically different, compilation would fail.

I demonstrate this on this small example:
https://github.com/Risto97/verilator_cmake_issue

A dummy file is provided during verilating:
https://github.com/Risto97/verilator_cmake_issue/blob/master/vsrcs/apb_subsystem_dummy.v
It only defines module that has the same ports, so the verilating doesnt fail

The real file that should generated is the next:
https://github.com/Risto97/verilator_cmake_issue/blob/master/vsrcs/apb_subsystem.v

To run the example do this: (tested on cmake 3.25, lower the version if older)
```
git clone https://github.com/Risto97/verilator_cmake_issue.git
cd verilator_cmake_issue
mkdir build
cd build
cmake ../
make 
```
The compilation will fail now:
```
/usr/bin/ld: CMakeFiles/vlt.dir/CMakeFiles/vlt.dir/Vtb.dir/Vtb___024root__DepSet_ha183790c__0__Slow.cpp.o: in function `Vtb___024root___eval_settle(Vtb___024root*)':
Vtb___024root__DepSet_ha183790c__0__Slow.cpp:(.text+0x9a): undefined reference to `Vtb___024root___eval_triggers__stl(Vtb___024root*)'
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
```
As the Vtb.cmake is the one that is made for dummy file.

### Reconfigure project when file changes:
One solution is to rerun the configuration of the project when the file changes.
If you uncomment the line below the compilation will be successful. But I am not a big fan of this solution
https://github.com/Risto97/verilator_cmake_issue/blob/master/CMakeLists.txt#L46

## Use cmake external project
My next solution is the one that I am currently using, and that is to use verilate function in an cmake external project.
This way external project can depend on the targets that generate verilog files.
This way verilate external project is not configured until files are generated with their final content.

Basically this is the external project CMakeLists.txt
https://github.com/Risto97/VeriSC/blob/master/cmake/verilator/verilator_ext_prj.cmake
And this is how I include it in the main project:
https://github.com/Risto97/VeriSC/blob/master/cmake/verilator/verilator.cmake#L32
It is not ideal but it was the best solution I found.
